conversation.user: I'm building a portfolio website. I know basic HTML/CSS but need to learn React. I want to finish in 6 weeks. I can put in about 1 hour on weekdays, 3 hours on weekends.

expectedAI.diagnosis: 6 weeks is too optimistic for React beginner. React alone takes 2+ weeks to feel comfortable. Hofstadter's Law: always 2-3× longer than estimated. Weekly budget: 5×60 + 2×180 = 660min. Weekend 3hr blocks are highest-leverage time — primary build sessions.
expectedAI.strategy: 10-week plan (8 weeks work + 2 weeks buffer). Week 1-2: React basics (Hooks, components, props, state) + To-do app. Week 3: project setup + landing page → DEPLOY #1. Week 4-5: About + Projects pages + navigation. Week 6-7: interactions + responsive + styling → DEPLOY #2. Week 8: polish + testing + content. Week 9-10: buffer + FINAL DEPLOY. Stuck protocol: 2+ days blocked → simplify scope, try ChatGPT/SO, take a day off.
expectedAI.honestAssessment: 6 weeks is not achievable for a React beginner building a portfolio. 10 weeks (8 work + 2 buffer) is the realistic minimum. Buffer weeks always get used.

alternatives.label: 8주 최소 플랜
alternatives.change: 6주 → 8주 (버퍼 없음), 전적인 헌신 + 주요 블로커 없을 때만 가능

mustIdentify: 6 weeks too optimistic for React beginner
diagnosis.mustReflectInPlan: 10-week realistic timeline with deploy milestones and buffer weeks

emotion.toneExpected: honest but encouraging; timeline reality check without discouraging; deploy milestones as motivation
